name: Ruta de las Estrellas — circular desde Abengibre
region_intro: Hiking around Abengibre offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Júcar river, which carves canyons and creates varied terrain. The region features a mix of rural paths, riverine environments, and rolling hills. Hikers can explore routes that traverse open countryside and follow the course of the river, providing a range of elevation changes. The area is characterized by its natural beauty and opportunities for outdoor activity. Best hiking trails around Abengibre The most popular hiking route is Entrebarrancos Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and views along the river. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruta de las Estrellas — circular desde Abengibre, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This circular trail starts directly from Abengibre and explores the surrounding rural landscape. Local hikers also love the Castle of Alcalá del Júcar – Alcalá del Júcar loop from Alcalá del Júcar, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through historic areas and offering views of the castle, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Hiking around Abengibre is defined by river valleys, rural paths, and varied hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Abengibre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Abengibre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Abengibre?
A: There are 17 hiking trails around Abengibre, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls to more challenging routes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hikes in Abengibre?
A: The hiking trails around Abengibre cater to various abilities. There are 6 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes, ensuring options for every hiker.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect on hikes around Abengibre?
A: Hiking around Abengibre offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Júcar river, featuring canyons, rural paths, and rolling hills. You'll traverse open countryside and follow the river's course, providing varied elevation changes and natural beauty.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking trails near Abengibre?
A: Yes, Abengibre has 6 easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10348646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alcalá del Júcar – Alcalá del Júcar loop from Alcalá del Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.1 miles (6.6 km) and leads through historic areas with castle views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting directly from Abengibre?
A: Yes, for those looking for a circular route directly from the town, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068349220/ruta-de-las-estrellas-circular-desde-abengibre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de las Estrellas — circular desde Abengibre&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. This moderate 7.2-mile (11.6 km) path explores the surrounding rural landscape.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails around Abengibre?
A: Many trails offer stunning natural attractions. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5971445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morrón – Júcar River Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The area is rich in river valleys and natural monuments.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on the hikes in Abengibre?
A: Yes, several routes offer excellent viewpoints, particularly those along the Júcar River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight, providing panoramic vistas of the river and its surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots or natural pools near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can find natural pools along the Júcar River. Specifically, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5738143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pools at the Júcar Dam&lt;/a&gt; are a popular spot for a refreshing dip, often accessible from nearby hiking paths.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Abengibre?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river views, and the opportunities to explore both rural landscapes and dramatic gorges.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the Abengibre area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1074843258/las-eras-canon-del-jucar-el-morron-tolosa-circular-desde-alcala-del-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Eras, Cañon del Júcar, El Morrón, Tolosa — circular desde Alcala del Júcar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.6-mile (23.4 km) route that takes you through the Júcar Canyon and offers significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Abengibre?
A: Moderate hikes in Abengibre vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075865248/entrebarrancos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrebarrancos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a popular moderate route, is 5.6 miles (9.0 km) and typically takes about 2 hours 40 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any hikes that follow the Júcar River for an extended period?
A: Yes, many trails closely follow the Júcar River, offering beautiful riverside scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086599807/jorquera-to-la-recuaja-circular-following-the-rio-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorquera to La Recuaja — circular following the Río Júcar&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, covering 9.8 miles (15.8 km) and providing extensive views along the river's course.
tips: The water from the jets comes out very cold. It is not drinkable.

The church dates from the 17th century and is built in the Baroque style. One of the most outstanding aspects of the church is its tower, which has a height of 40 meters and a decoration that mixes baroque and neoclassical elements. The tower is considered one of the most emblematic monuments of Abengibre.
